pr.title: Enforce plugin runtime guardrails
pr.body:
Several plugin-system guardrails existed in code but weren't actually enforced: permission checks logged and continued, manifest validation never blocked loading, `version_constraint` was ignored, `min_core_version` used lexicographic string compare, failed `setup()` left plugins stuck in `initializing`, vendored deps could shadow core packages via `sys.path[0]`, and `auto_enable_on_install` defaulted to `True`.

### Changes

- **`context.py` — permission enforcement**
  - `subscribe()` / `emit()` now `log.error(...)` and `raise PermissionError(...)` with the documented message format instead of warning + silent skip.

- **`manager.py` — lifecycle & validation**
  - `_safe_async_setup` (and the sync branch of `_execute_setup`) set `status = "failed"` on exception and emit `plugin:setup_failed` directly on the global `bus`, bypassing plugin permissions.
  - `_validate_manifest` returns `(issues, critical_issues)`. Any issues → entry `status = "degraded"`; critical issues (incompatible/invalid `min_core_version`, missing dependencies) additionally short-circuit `_execute_setup`. Missing `description`/`icon` remain warn-only.
  - `min_core_version` compared via `packaging.version.Version`; invalid formats reported as critical.
  - `_check_dependencies_met` evaluates `dep.version_constraint` with `packaging.specifiers.SpecifierSet` once the dependency is active; mismatch or invalid spec returns `False`.
  - Vendor path switched from `sys.path.insert(0, …)` to `sys.path.append(…)` so plugin deps cannot shadow core modules.

- **`models.py` — safer default**
  - `ModuleManifest.auto_enable_on_install` default flipped from `True` to `False`.

- **`plugin_service.py` — install-time identity check**
  - New `_verify_plugin_identity(staging_path, expected_id)` loads the staged `entrypoint.py` in an isolated spec and compares `manifest.id`.
  - On `upgrade`, the installed plugin's id is read first and verified against the staged archive before the atomic swap; mismatch aborts the install and emits `plugin:install_failed` with `error="identity_mismatch"`. No public signatures changed.

### Notes for reviewers

- Fix 1 is a behavioral change for any plugin currently relying on silent permission failures — calls will now raise. Intentional, per spec.
- Fix 7 changes the default for new plugin installs; existing `PluginState` rows are unaffected.
- The bonus identity check only runs on `upgrade=True` paths where an expected id can be derived from the installed copy; fresh installs have no prior identity to compare against.
pr.author: Copilot
